subject: steps-private-research v18 HD presentation rejection
rights_scope: Research and test evidence only; no formal game release is authorized by this record.
human_observation: The border changed from a square to a rounded rectangle while the game content inside remained effectively the same enlarged pixel construction.
root_causes: The production renderer derived all target geometry from final 128 by 128 framebuffer connected components instead of source semantic map, sprite, primitive, and text routes.

The contour helper treated 0.28 as a fraction of each edge rather than an absolute source-pixel displacement, moving a 40-pixel edge endpoint by 11.2 source pixels and rounding source-square frames into capsules.
Source framebuffer glyph contours remained in the generic geometry pass and fixed-font Text was then drawn again, allowing doubled and blurred typography.
The map classifier did not filter cells by the source layer mask and sprite flags, so marker tiles and the two ordered map layers could be misclassified as visible content.
The previous private topology verifier explicitly required final-framebuffer geometry and prohibited semantic scene/tile reconstruction, locking the false-HD approach into the local gate.
required_systemic_fix: Build content geometry from source-ordered map, sprite, primitive, and PRINT semantics; use the framebuffer only for visibility, read-modify-write effects, and review oracle duties.
Preserve the exact square composition and source draw order, including flag-7 map, entities, player, flag-0 map, effects, frame, and HUD.
Require per-scene content render routes and reject shell-only, final-framebuffer-topology, and cosmetic-smoothing-only candidates.
Require measurable material layers, authored internal detail, or source-timed animation/effect enrichment in addition to continuous contours and density-aware sampling.
Return Steps to offline draft and regenerate every runtime, capture, packet, and lifecycle artifact before another human review.
